items.rationale: Viewed as crypto's clearest product-market fit and the wedge to onboard billions; infrastructure and cost curves finally converging with merchant savings (~30%) and cross-border efficiency.
items.age_note: Payments a founding goal restated as imminent through 2024; intensified into 'killer app' framing by Sept 2024

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoin payments could be the answer to how we bring billions of people onchain.
Payments has been one of the goals of crypto from the beginning. The pieces are finally coming together.

items.rationale: Seen as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a democratizing developer primitive; falling prover overhead expands the application surface, though security/bugs remain the gating risk.
items.age_note: Building since 2023 (Lasso/Jolt Aug 2023); Jolt Alpha shipped Apr 2024, still called 'under hyped' Jun 2024

citations.quote: I still think ZK is under hyped.
This is a significant contribution toward the endgame solution for blockchain scalability, and of course the implementation is fully open source

items.rationale: Framed as the future of consumer crypto — user-owned physical networks (energy, telecom, transport) that centralized models can't replicate; energy seen as needing decentralization, not just wanting it.
items.age_note: Emerged as a dominant 2024 thesis; Daylight Energy Series A led Jul 2024

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN
Energy is one of the few non-crypto industries that doesn't just want decentralization, but needs it

items.rationale: Blockchains positioned as counterbalance to centralized AI and as the coordination/provenance layer for an explosion of AI-generated content; only crypto seen as a serious solution to deepfakes.
items.age_note: Theme since 2023 (Gensyn Jun 2023); sharpened around content provenance and IP in 2024

citations.quote: We need an open, global coordination layer in order for AI generated content to thrive. And this is exactly what blockchains can provide.
I'm talking specifically about decentralized consensus/blockchain... there's no serious solution to the deepfake problem that doesn't use it.

items.rationale: An onchain IP/licensing layer to rebuild the creator-platform economic covenant broken by generative AI; described as the most exciting infrastructure innovation seen in 15 years of IP market-design interest.
items.age_note: Interest since 2023 (Story Protocol Sept 2023); $80M Series B led Aug 2024

citations.quote: I don't think I've ever seen an infrastructure innovation that excites me as much as @StoryProtocol.

items.rationale: Rollup-centric roadmap (implicit sharding) plus EigenLayer-style restaking and shared sequencers seen as unlocking 100x innovation; non-native DA viewed as required near-term to hit real throughput.
items.age_note: Consistent through 2023-2024; EigenLayer Series B Feb 2024, Espresso Series B Mar 2024; urgency restated Sept 2024

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges
Non-native DA is clearly required to scale Ethereum to its full potential in the short term.
Founders are choosing their infrastructure based on present and future expected capacity. Ethereum cannot afford to slow down.

items.rationale: Held as a prerequisite for mainstream adoption; new tools (private smart contracts, web proofs, enclaves) expand the design space — but must be built to mitigate illicit-finance concerns.
items.age_note: Mainstream-privacy thesis stated 2023, reinforced as Aleo went live Sept 2024 and zkTLS flagged underrated Jul 2024

citations.quote: It's hard to imagine crypto ever reaching mainstream adoption without privacy.
zkTLS / zkEmail is an underrated category in crypto.
Aleo network is live

items.rationale: Games seen as a historical source of software innovation and a leading driver of throughput/infrastructure demand; composability brings player ownership and modding to the foreground.
items.age_note: Proof of Play backed 2023, cited as #1 chain by Mgas/s Aug 2024

citations.quote: #1 chain measured by Mgas/s (the correct measure for throughput) is @ProofOfPlay — by a long shot.

items.rationale: Open, interoperable, user-owned social protocols seen as the cure for web2 platform lock-in and deplatforming; value persists at protocol level even at scale.
items.age_note: Long-running thesis (2023), restated Sept 2024

citations.quote: The need for competition in the social media market is a big part of why I'm excited about decentralized social media protocols

items.rationale: New legal entities (DUNA) and 'Machiavellian' governance designs seen as unlocking accountable, scalable decentralized coordination and de-risking DAO members.
items.age_note: Emphasized late 2023 into 2024; DUNA framed as native DAO entity Mar 2024

citations.quote: DAOs finally have a native, tailor made entity

items.rationale: Seen as actively setting the industry back — deterring serious founders, arming hostile regulators, and reframing crypto as a casino; technically uninteresting and net-negative once externalities counted.
items.age_note: Anti-casino stance since 2023 ('we don't need the casino'); peaked into a sustained campaign Mar-Apr 2024

citations.quote: We don't need the casino. We never did. It's the primary force holding the space back.
Memecoins alter how the public, regulators, and entrepreneurs see crypto. At best, it looks like a risky casino.

items.rationale: The house's loudest concern — viewed as a politicized, unbounded expansion of securities law (now reaching NFTs/OpenSea and DEX frontends) that fosters uncertainty, drives builders offshore, and threatens US leadership.
items.age_note: Persistent since 2023; still escalating with OpenSea Wells notice Aug 2024 and US geofencing advice Sept 2024

citations.quote: This is a brazen example of the SEC's use of lawfare to chill innovation.
regulation-by-enforcement is no substitute for formal rulemaking and public comment processes

items.rationale: Fast paths to liquidity seen as an existential drag on innovation, producing vaporware and retail harm (SPAC analogy); disclosure alone deemed insufficient — long holding periods required.
items.age_note: High-FDV/low-float concern Feb 2024; sharpened into 'existential threat' framing Sept 2024

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
Agree that high FDV/low float is a significant problem, but longer/gradual lockups are a better approach

items.rationale: Warns founders against fixed non-dilutable token allocations, short lockups designed for VC dumping, and VCs charging tokens for 'launch services' — all misalign incentives and harm the industry.
items.age_note: Campaign from Dec 2023 through Aug 2024

citations.quote: Fixed token allocations are predatory.
If a VC pushes to shorten your token lockup... what they're really telling you is "we intend to dump our bags"

items.rationale: Concern that law-ignoring privacy tools (e.g., Samourai-style) invite government bans that would gut crypto's utility; system design must mitigate money-laundering/sanctions risk to reach critical mass.
items.age_note: Tornado Cash concerns 2023; restated forcefully Apr 2024

citations.quote: if crypto privacy becomes synonymous with infinite money laundering/terrorist financing/sanctions avoidance we'll never reach critical mass.

items.rationale: Anticipates an internet where most content is AI-generated and unverifiable by default; absent provenance infrastructure this erodes trust — framed as both threat and crypto opportunity.
items.age_note: Stated Dec 2023, reiterated through 2024

citations.quote: we aren't far from the internet devolving into a place where *most* content has been generated
The "mystery meat" scenario seems unavoidable

items.rationale: Research shows flight-to-safety dynamics and liquidity withdrawal during stress amplify run risk on 'less safe' stablecoins; concentrating power in one issuer (or CBDC) undermines decentralization.
items.age_note: Academic run-risk work Sept 2023; concentration warning Feb 2024

citations.quote: Concentrated power in the hands of any single stablecoin issuer undermines decentralization across web3.
this notion of competition between stable coins during this period of stress in May impose this additional increased run risk

items.rationale: Watching Lido's >1/3 stake share, shared-sequencer MEV monopolies, and restaking 'shocks' that could cascade; centralization treated as gravity that incentives must actively counteract.
items.age_note: Lido concern Oct 2023; restaking-security framework Aug 2024

citations.quote: When is a restaking network "robustly secure," meaning that a small "shock"... cannot enable a catastrophic attack?
Centralization is like gravity, and if we don't tip the incentives in favor of decentralization, we're never going to reach escape velocity.

items.rationale: Expected to be a deflationary currency long-term (post-merge economic security); SEC's ETF approval treated as formal recognition that ETH is a decentralized commodity, not a security — clearing a regulatory beachhead.
items.age_note: Security-status fight resolved in 2024: SEC 'capitulated' Jan 2024, ETF approvals May 2024; deflation thesis Sept 2024

citations.quote: long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency
These approvals enshrine the SEC's position in 2018 - ETH is not a security.

items.rationale: Treated as a relatively safe, transparent, fiat-backed stablecoin and likely not a security (issuer isn't running a yield scheme); core rail for the payments thesis and a flight-to-safety beneficiary.
items.age_note: Distinguished from yield-bearing/algorithmic coins Dec 2023; reinforced by stablecoin 'killer app' framing 2024

citations.quote: USDC is likely okay, but yield bearing stablecoins should take note.
the flight to safety here was basically towards either die or usdc both much more transparent stable coins

items.rationale: Equated to chain emails — purely speculative, no protocol/art/game underneath, memetic value only; their grift damages tech progress and regulatory standing.
items.age_note: Consistent 2023-2024 hostility, peaking Apr 2024

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.
there now isn't any pretense of actual underlying value... they seek to entice through hopes of memetic value.

items.rationale: Flagged for reserve opacity and asset-quality concerns; during stress the market questions Tether's ability to maintain the peg even as it absorbs flight from regulatory-exposed coins.
items.age_note: Stated in Sept 2023 stablecoin research; not prominently restated since

citations.quote: there were a lot of concerns about the quality of the assets of tether and which also was particularly not transparent about the quality of these assets

items.rationale: Viewed as prone to death spirals (pegs rely on belief in created tokens); regulatory consensus is against them, and UST is cited as the textbook collapse and securities-law violation.
items.age_note: UST already collapsed (2022); analyzed Sept 2023 and Dec 2023 — concern persists for the category

citations.quote: as we've seen in the context of Terra um these things could lead um very quickly to death Spirals and unravel
Terra was both the issuer/promoter of UST and the operator of Anchor... and its bullshit 20% yield, which also wasn't decentralized

risk_regime.rationale: The house is actively deploying into infrastructure (EigenLayer, Espresso, Story, Daylight, Aleo ecosystem) and points to adoption metrics at all-time highs (State of Crypto Index, mobile wallets, DEX volume) and improving UX/regulatory tooling — 'never a better time to build.' But conviction is fundamentals-and-builder-focused, NOT market/speculation-on: they explicitly reject the memecoin casino, warn against fast secondary liquidity, and treat US regulatory hostility as severe enough to geofence the US for some products. Net: structurally bullish on the technology and long-term builders, tactically defensive on speculation and US regulation.

notes: Recency weighting: 2024 Q2-Q3 statements dominate. Clear reversals recorded — ETH's securities status flipped from open question to 'commodity' after the Jan 2024 SEC futures non-denial and May 2024 ETF approvals (now bullish/resolved). The anti-speculation ('casino') and pro-decentralization stances are consistent across the whole 18 months and intensifying, not reversing. DePIN, crypto-x-AI, programmable IP, and zkVMs are newer 2024 obsessions; privacy, payments, L2 scaling, and decentralized social are persistent theses that were restated recently (so graded grounded). The deepest single concern is US regulation-by-enforcement, which colors the team's entire risk posture and several token-launch behaviors (lockups, US exclusion). Stablecoin run-risk and Tether/algorithmic-coin concerns lean on Sept 2023 research and are partly persisted rather than freshly restated. Author voices differ within the corpus (legal/Miles Jennings on regulation; Chris Dixon/economists on networks; Justin Thaler/Dan Boneh on ZK; investment leads on DePIN/AI) but converge on the consensus above; no material intra-house contradictions found on the headline views as of 2024-09-30.
